basics.name: Atilla Çelikgil
basics.summary: PhD candidate in Biology at Martin Luther University of Halle-Wittenberg, researching wild bee responses to urban ecosystems using comparative genomics. Research assistant with experience in molecular biology, population genetics, and biodiversity conservation.

work.name: Martin Luther University of Halle-Wittenberg
work.summary: Project: A comparative genomic approach of wild bee responses to urban ecosystems. Supervisors: Panagiotis Theodorou, PhD & Robert Paxton, PhD

work.name: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
work.summary: Project: Screening undiagnosed bone fragments from Flores, Indonesia, in search for hobbit (Homo floresiensis) DNA. Supervisor: Matthias Meyer, PhD

work.name: Middle East Technical University (METU)
work.summary: Project: Genetic structure -geography relations and genome-wide selection in European honeybees of Asia minor (Apis mellifera). Supervisor: C. Can Bilgin, PhD

work.name: Martin Luther University of Halle-Wittenberg
work.summary: Projects: Identification of cryptic species of Euglossa: Genomic analysis of odorant receptor genes; Assessing the susceptibility of European honeybees (Apis mellifera) to Deformed wing virus-A (DWV-A). Supervisor: Robert Paxton, PhD

publications.name: Prey and prejudice: predation by the European bee-eater Merops apiaster has species-specific effects on the ecology and genetics of bumblebees
publications.summary: Belinda Kahnt, Andreas Etel, Felix Jarosch, Oliver Schweiger, Atilla Çelikgil, Panagiotis Theodorou

publications.name: Urban intensity reshapes Hymenoptera communities and decouples nesting from reproduction

teaching.course: Group Research Colloquium in General Zoology
teaching.description: Co-organised and supervised in a research colloquium on student-led zoology projects; provided mentoring and feedback on scientific presentations.
